prose:
**Water frontier-orbital HOMO–LUMO gap** — DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, exact integrals, no density fitting) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: single-point calculation on the supplied water.xyz (not relaxed at DFT). SCF converged after 7 cycles. 

**Headline result:** HOMO–LUMO gap = **8.9406687 eV** (Koopmans-style orbital energy difference: HOMO at −8.6037183 eV, LUMO at +0.33695037 eV). This represents the vertical ionization potential (cost to remove an electron from the HOMO) minus the vertical electron affinity (cost to add an electron to the LUMO), and is distinct from the true excitation energy (which would require a correlated response calculation).

Frontier orbital details: HOMO-2 = −14.444 eV, HOMO-1 = −10.679 eV, HOMO = −8.604 eV, LUMO = +0.337 eV, LUMO+1 = +2.391 eV, LUMO+2 = +9.597 eV. No warnings reported. Calculation integrity verified: all checks passed.
